content: Contraindications: severe renal impairment (eGFR <20-25 depending on agent and indication), dialysis, history of serious hypersensitivity, type 1 diabetes (DKA risk). Warnings/precautions: diabetic ketoacidosis (may occur with normal glucose - 'euglycemic DKA'), acute kidney injury, urosepsis/pyelonephritis, genital mycotic infections, hypotension, lower limb amputation (canagliflozin signal), bone fracture risk. Monitoring: baseline and periodic renal function, volume status, signs/symptoms of DKA (nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, malaise), genital infections, foot exams in high-risk patients. Common adverse effects: genital mycotic infections (5-10%, more common in women), urinary tract infections, volume depletion, increased urination. Rare but serious: Fournier's gangrene (necrotizing fasciitis of perineum), euglycemic DKA (hold 3-4 days before surgery). Drug interactions: diuretics (additive volume depletion), insulin/sulfonylureas (hypoglycemia risk when combined).
